page: Microsoft Azure D64d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 35 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server includes 2458 GB of local storage, making it suitable for workloads requiring fast local disk access. In benchmark evaluations, the D64d_v4 demonstrates strong performance in memory bandwidth tests, specifically in large block read-only and write operations, placing in the top 25%. Passmark memory scores and database operations also rank in the top 25%, indicating high efficiency for data-intensive tasks. However, single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, fall within the average middle 50% range.

While the server excels in memory throughput and database operations, it exhibits limitations in specific computational tasks. Large language model text generation benchmarks show weak to poor performance, particularly for text generation using 135M and 7B models. Given these characteristics, the D64d_v4 is highly suitable for relational databases, enterprise applications, and general-purpose workloads requiring substantial local storage and high memory bandwidth, rather than specialized AI inference or high-compute tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D64d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 2458 GB of local storage and supports AVX-512 instructions. Benchmark data indicates strong performance in memory bandwidth and database operations, both ranking in the top 25%. Conversely, CPU-bound tasks and LLM text generation show average to weak results. This resource profile makes the D64d_v4 well-suited for database hosting, enterprise applications, and general-purpose workloads requiring high memory throughput and local storage capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, delivering strong memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64d_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for database workloads.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated vCPUs, high-capacity local storage, and strong memory bandwidth for database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 256 GB of system memory
Strong memory bandwidth and database operation performance
Average single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark results
Best suited for relational databases and enterprise applications
